title: Delicate Mediterranean Chicken Tagine
description: A citrusy and tender Mediterranean dish featuring chicken cooked in a rich tagine sauce, perfect for a comforting sick day meal. This halal-friendly recipe incorporates palm sugar and nam tan pip for added depth of flavor.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the crushed long fermentation dough and cook until lightly toasted, stirring frequently.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken thighs and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the palm sugar, nam tan pip, lemon juice, chicken broth, salt, and black pepper, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the Dutch oven and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the Dutch oven from the oven and let it cool slightly before serving.
Serve the chicken and sauce over couscous or rice, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
